level_signals: Owns full lifecycle of employee experience programs
Reports into People Ops Lead (not a leadership role)
Expected to design programs at scale, manage vendors, and partner cross-functionally

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You've designed and owned employee experience or culture programs at a fast-scaling company — you've built programs that people actually remember, not just check-the-box ones
You're decisive and action-oriented; you're comfortable moving before the brief is perfect
You know when to go be incredibly personalized and when to design for scale

You have a clear sense of what's tasteful — you can make something warm and meaningful without it tipping into performative or generic, and you have strong instincts for what fits a culture versus what will land wrong
Your writing is clean and your instincts for internal communications are sharp
You work well across teams you don't control — you know how to partner with EAs, Workplace, Comms, and leadership and move things forward
Vendor management experience for swag, gifts, or event production at scale

scope.surface: Company-wide employee experience programs — milestone gifting, recognition programs, events, swag, ERG formation, internal communications, and culture rituals
scope.business_model: B2B SaaS / AI legal technology platform
primary_metrics_inferred: Employee program participation and engagement rates
EE program spend vs. quality outcomes
core_responsibilities: Own the full lifecycle of employee experience programs — from milestone gifting and work anniversaries to company-wide recognition like Harvey Honors and the annual holiday program
Maintain the company-wide events calendar and design and drive Harvey's community and social programming
Orchestrate event execution across offices — directing the program, coordinating with teams in each office to bring each piece to life
Own Harvey's swag and brand touchpoints — vendor relationships, item curation, and a gifting strategy that scales without feeling generic
Manage all communications for EE programs — announcements, invites, and follow-ups — in a voice that drives engagement and reflects how Harvey talks
Partner with the People Programs manager on employee survey design, eNPS analysis, and program reporting; use data to inform what you build next
Partner with executive leadership to ensure visibility and engagement at company-wide moments
Track EE program spend across gifting, events, and swag; manage vendor relationships with an eye on quality and cost
Build the infrastructure for ERG formation as Harvey scales
Build guidelines and resources that enable the team to recognize their people consistently — birthdays, work anniversaries, team wins
Build repeatable playbooks for recurring programs

green_flags: Clear scope of ownership — not a supporting role, but full program ownership
Role reports into People Ops Lead with named cross-functional partners, indicating organizational clarity
Harvey has strong product-market fit signals (1000+ customers, 60+ countries) suggesting financial stability
Responsibilities are specific and operational, not vague
red_flags: No location type specified — unclear if remote, hybrid, or onsite; no cities listed for this role
Visa sponsorship and work authorization not addressed anywhere in the JD
No years of experience stated — seniority expectations are implicit only
Equity not mentioned despite being a common component at AI startups at this stage
No mention of whether this is a new role or backfill, making team context harder to assess

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range and scope of responsibilities)
scope.business_model (inferred from company description — AI legal technology B2B platform)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly stated in JD)
compensation.location_pay_differential (inferred from 'Depending on your location' language near comp range)
ambiguities: Location type is never specified — the JD mentions 'across offices' suggesting multi-office coordination but does not state whether the employee must be onsite, hybrid, or can be remote.
Visa sponsorship and work authorization are not addressed in the JD body.
No explicit years of experience requirement — all requirements are qualitative, making hard/soft classification rely on the unconditional 'What You Have' framing.
